CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT, value_a DOUBLE, value_b DOUBLE);
SELECT sys.covar_pop(value_a, value_a * 2) FROM test_table WHERE value_a IS NOT NULL;
SELECT sys.covar_pop(a.value_a, b.value_b) FROM test_table a JOIN test_table b ON a.id = b.id WHERE a.value_a IS NOT NULL AND b.value_b IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, sys.covar_pop(value_a, value_b) OVER (PARTITION BY id) FROM test_table;
CREATE TABLE second_table (group_id INT, group_value_a DOUBLE, group_value_b DOUBLE);
SELECT t1.id, sys.covar_pop(t1.value_a, t2.group_value_b) FROM test_table t1 JOIN second_table t2 ON t1.id = t2.group_id WHERE t1.value_a IS NOT NULL AND t2.group_value_b IS NOT NULL;
SELECT sys.covar_pop(value_a, CASE WHEN value_b IS NULL THEN 0 ELSE value_b END) FROM test_table;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_table AS SELECT value_a, value_b FROM test_table WHERE value_a > 1.0;
SELECT sys.covar_pop(value_a, value_b) FROM temp_table WHERE value_b < 5.0;
SELECT AVG(sys.covar_pop(value_a, value_b)) OVER () FROM test_table;
SELECT COUNT(*), sys.covar_pop(value_a, value_b) FROM test_table GROUP BY id HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
